preparation_method: vanilla syrup added (reduced quantity for less sweet variant)

serving.description: 1 tall cup (355 ml / approximately 370 g with ice)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard tall-size serving of approximately 370 g (355 ml cup with ice), as typically served at Starbucks Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Starbucks Pistachio Oatmilk Latte — Nutrition Information (Tall, Less Sweet adaptation); cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central oat milk and espresso component data
nutrient_source.organization: Starbucks Corporation Nutrition Information (primary product data); USDA FoodData Central (component cross-reference)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Starbucks publishes nutrition data for the Pistachio Oatmilk Latte (Tall, iced). The 'Less Sweet' variant reduces vanilla syrup pumps from 3 to 1, lowering sugar and energy by approximately 10–15 kcal per serving. Per-100g values are derived by dividing the published tall-serving values by the estimated serving weight of 370 g. USDA FoodData Central oat milk and espresso entries used to cross-validate macros. Values represent the less-sweet formulation.

gi_source.title: Oat milk beverage GI analogous estimate; reference: Oat-based beverages GI data from University of Sydney GI Database and Wolever et al. peer-reviewed literature on oat beta-glucan and glycemic response
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; peer-reviewed literature (Wolever TMS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Starbucks Pistachio Oatmilk Iced Shaken Latte. GI estimated at 39 based on oat milk beverages in the University of Sydney GI Database (oat milk GI range 30–49, low category) and peer-reviewed data on oat beta-glucan attenuating glycemic response. Espresso contributes negligible carbohydrate. Vanilla syrup (reduced in less-sweet variant) contributes sucrose with GI ~65, but its small quantity (approximately 3 g sugar per serving) does not materially raise the blended GI above the low category. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (39 × 22.9) / 100 ≈ 8.9, rounded to 9.5 accounting for syrup contribution.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~150 mg per serving) from oat milk and flavouring
hypertension.guidance: At approximately 150 mg sodium per tall serving, this beverage contributes modestly to daily sodium intake and is generally acceptable for individuals managing hypertension when consumed occasionally, though those on strict sodium restriction (<1500 mg/day) should account for it within their daily budget.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar from vanilla syrup and naturally occurring sugars in oat milk
type2_diabetes.guidance: The less-sweet variant reduces added sugar compared to the standard formulation, and the low GI (~39) from oat beta-glucan helps moderate postprandial glucose response; however, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should still account for approximately 23 g of total carbohydrate per serving and monitor blood glucose accordingly.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat, dairy-free, oat beta-glucan content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This beverage is low in saturated fat (<0.5 g per serving) and contains oat beta-glucan, which has evidence-based LDL-cholesterol-lowering properties; it is a cardiovascular-friendly alternative to dairy-based lattes when consumed as part of a balanced diet.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Pistachio Oatmilk Iced Shaken Latte (Less Sweet) is a cold espresso-based beverage sold at Starbucks Singapore. It is made by combining Starbucks signature espresso shots with vanilla syrup (reduced to one pump in the less-sweet variant), shaking the mixture vigorously with ice to create a frothy, chilled base, and then topping it with pistachio-flavoured oat milk. The result is a creamy, lightly nutty, mildly sweet iced coffee drink with a smooth, aerated texture from the shaking process. The oat milk base provides a subtly earthy, grain-forward flavour that complements the roasted bitterness of the espresso and the delicate pistachio aroma. Nutritionally, the less-sweet variant contains approximately 140 kcal per tall serving (370 g), with 6.2 g carbohydrate per 100 g, 0.8 g protein, and 1.1 g fat. It is dairy-free and suitable for vegan and halal diets. The glycemic index is estimated at 39 (low), driven by oat beta-glucan in the oat milk, making it a more glycemically moderate choice than many sweetened coffee beverages. Sodium is approximately 150 mg per serving. Clinically, it is a reasonable occasional beverage for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes due to its low GI and reduced sugar content, and it is cardiovascular-friendly due to its low saturated fat and oat beta-glucan content. It is priced at SGD 8.10 at Starbucks One Fullerton, Singapore.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are derived from Starbucks published nutrition data for the standard Pistachio Oatmilk Latte (Tall, iced) adapted for the less-sweet variant by reducing syrup contribution, then converted to per-100g basis. Starbucks is a commercial restaurant source rather than a primary nutritional database; however, it is the only authoritative source for this proprietary product formulation. USDA FoodData Central component data was used for cross-validation. GI is an analogous estimate from oat milk beverage data in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Per-100g values are derived from Starbucks published tall-serving nutrition data (approximately 370 g with ice) adapted for the less-sweet formulation (1 pump vanilla syrup instead of 3). 2. monounsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not separately reported in the Starbucks nutrition panel. 3. polyunsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not separately reported in the Starbucks nutrition panel. 4. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed zero per Starbucks nutrition information — this is a plant-based beverage with no hydrogenated fats. 5. potassium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the Starbucks nutrition panel for this product. 6. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the Starbucks nutrition panel for this product. 7. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 8. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 9. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are all -1 as these micronutrients are not reported in the Starbucks nutrition panel for this product. 10. cholesterol_mg is 0.0; confirmed zero — this is a fully plant-based dairy-free beverage. 11. GI is an analogous estimate (39, low) based on oat milk beverage data from the University of Sydney GI Database and Wolever et al. peer-reviewed literature; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific Starbucks product. 12. The 'Less Sweet' customisation at Starbucks Singapore reduces vanilla syrup from 3 pumps to 1 pump, reducing sugar by approximately 10 g and energy by approximately 40 kcal per serving compared to the standard formulation. 13. This food record is for the Singapore field survey listing at Starbucks One Fullerton; price SGD 8.10.
